title: `podman {FOO}` results in "cannot open: /proc/$NONEXISTENT_PID/ns/user" on fresh F32 install
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

`podman {FOO}` results in "cannot open: /proc/$NONEXISTENT_PID/ns/user" on fresh F32 install

Typing ANY podman command (except `version`) results in "cannot open: /proc/$PID/ns/user" where $PID doesn't exist in /proc.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install Fedora 32 release (KDE spin, if that matters) from scratch - nothing on the hard drive.
1. Log in
1. Try to run any podman command

**Describe the results you received:**

"cannot open: /proc/$PID/ns/user"

**Describe the results you expected:**

podman works

**Output of `podman version`:**

```text
Version:            1.9.1
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.14.2
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
cannot open: /proc/1995/ns/user
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-1.9.1-1.fc32.x86_64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Bare metal on a Lenovo Thinkpad laptop

comments.body: @giuseppe PTAL - looks like a pause process issue, but still happening on v1.9.1 (and a fresh install, at that)

I don't understand how this could have happened, but I had **neither** `/etc/subuid` **nor** `/etc/subgid`.

Simply following the ~~prompts~~ errors from running `podman system reset` (as NON-root) led me to `man 5 subuid` and creating the appropriate files.
I'm now getting this same error

> 
> 
> Podman version? Fedora release?

Fedora 32, podman 1.9.1

I'm getting the same error on Fedora 31 with podman 1.9.1

Every time I log out of my Gnome session and login again, I get this error. Running
```
rm /run/user/$UID/libpod/pause.pid
```
"fixed" the issue for me. It seems the pid file is not getting cleaned properly.
v1.9.2 was just released (should be in updates-testing now) - can you try that?
@mheon I cannot reproduce the issue on v1.9.2. Thanks!
I just had this error happen again. Maybe I need to make a habit of running `rm -rf /tmp/run-$(id -u)` whenever I want to use podman.
What Podman version? 1.9.2 doesn't appear to be in stable yet, but it should be landing there tomorrow (it's marked for stable as of today).
I'm still using 1.9.1. It's a simple enough problem to fix so I'll wait for 1.9.2 to be released to stable. The trouble is I don't know how to reproduce the error, it just happens sometimes.

> I'm still using 1.9.1. It's a simple enough problem to fix so I'll wait for 1.9.2 to be released to stable. The trouble is I don't know how to reproduce the error, it just happens sometimes.

I think the easiest reproducer is to run `killall podman` as rootless and it will also kill the pause process.

dnf -y update --enablerepo=update-testing podman

And you will get the new version.
